title: SOCIAL MEDIA IS AN ESCAPE FOR MANY- the choice because of the algorithm can make the escape even worse, causing anxiety, division, and declining mental health
tags: Social, social strategies, Marketing Campaign Idea, marketing, Brand positioning, Content ideas for social
notes:
https://youtu.be/u1U_JBut2-c?is=C7_SqXFtN0nVhAs1

I was watching YouTube, and it reminded me of something we ask people during the onboarding process: why they come to YouTube, and really why they're using social media. We know many people use it as an escape, and people choose different types of escapes. Some people choose to:
- work out
- go for a run
- read
- do positive things that will be positive in their lives


Other people choose to go to social media and doom scroll, escape, and unfortunately, because of the algorithm and its addictive quality, it sucks them in and makes them waste a tremendous amount of time. In the process, it feeds them all this garbage, mindless doom scrolling, and also negativity, anxiety-based, and violence stuff about war, death, and politics, which causes their mental health to deteriorate and robs them of the time they could use to positively impact their lives.

Before Signal, people really didn't have a choice to filter out the garbage. That's by design, whether it's Facebook, Instagram, X, Twitter, TikTok, YouTube, etc., but now they have a choice. 

With Signal, we're making people aware and now giving them a choice. If they're going to go to social media (which we know is hard for people to avoid, for example, because YouTube is the number one platform out there), they will eventually consume news on other platforms as well. We also want to filter and protect people from that, and give them positive information, educational information, things they can learn, and things they can laugh at that can better their lives and make them feel better. That's what Signal is about, and we want to make sure we relay that in our messaging across the website, emails, the waitlist, pre-order Kickstarter content, and blogs, and in content we post everywhere.

Let's do whatever research we need to on this, create the positioning statements we need, and discuss this at our next stand-up to see if you have any questions.

We also wanna take consideration how this will impact how we’re creating a list of videos for user to see, and then also as we look to suggest signal channels to people that will be another layer of recommendations based upon matching the criteria of what people chose and then what people watch and then another layer is what we can suggest to people other people’s channels. As an example, I foresee the opportunity for someone to create for example a calisthenics fitness channel with the capability for someone to search for YouTube on calisthenics click add to my channel create that channel similar to how Pinterest works or maybe similar services where someone’s curating content. If you think about it, this is done also automatically right by some services like if you look at the news when you go to sports aggregate all the news there are already right and a few then drool down further into NFL then it’s all related to NFL if it’s World Cup it World Cup you can also drill down one level lower. Let’s see NFL like Dallas Cowboys content. In our case, I foresee a capability in a feature where someone could create specific channels or sub channels of niche content as well right or someone wants to put a whole bunch of videos together from different creators, Even and so we’ll have a kind of a search functionality and similar to someone adding it to a playlist, which is kind of something you could do today in YouTube, but then being able to share that playlist or that channel naturally, what the type of functional we want to be able to do right when we talk about sharing someone’s channel sharing someone’s with the channel Mak signal Paige and then being able to share this specific niche categories or channels cause you have a signal page I guess, and then you have channels within the page  so we want to be able to have a functionality where you know when we suggest things to people people be able to maybe have a search function goes and get the content people be able to just add plus plus plus add those things to a channel or maybe you say here the recommended you know videos shorts long, whatever based on what they search for already using the filters and exclusion filters  

Innocence were empowering people to create these similar to again a YouTube uses playlist, but we’re gonna call him channel sub channels I guess similar to playlist where people gonna be able to not just share them, but we’re gonna be able to recommend them to people as the example if someone really likes calisthenics and we see that they watch a bunch of calisthenics videos so here’s a you know a channel some channels on calisthenics that we may think they may like and they can kind of click on it, go to it, and then add that channel to their signal page.  Like in the past, I have created and I still do it. I probably have. I don’t know couple hundred plus playlist that I have created in order to organize content on my YouTube and I guess I could share my playlist with someone but they have no power ability. I don’t believe to add that playlist to their YouTube page or subscribe to it? So this kind of flips it to where I have spent a lot of time curating playlists.  So now we wanna empower people to be able to find videos find signal pages and find signal channels similar to playlist that people can subscribe to.  Let’s have a chat about this in our next standup.

title: Search intent feature
tags: PRODUCT ENHANCEMENT, PRODUCT FEATURE, Differentiator
notes: So one of the big frustrations I have and I think a lot of people have is when you go to, for example YouTube and you search for something and you get the answer you watch a video or two, but then you keep getting videos again and again and again and again of what you just wanted some quick information on and now you have it so you don’t need to keep getting deluged by all this additional content that you’ve never asked for. So the idea is to put an intent selector maybe you can click on it when you go to search within our search functionality within signal so that if someone’s just searching for something that they want quickly get information on today or want to continuously get updated information on into the future until they change their mind. As an example, I was looking up information on Carl Jung’s red book. I watched a couple of videos a couple of shorts, but then the rest of the day he kept bombarding me with more and more  carl jung videos. So again I wanna add a feature that allows for people to select the videos are searching for if it’s I don’t know what you call it a quick momentary search not an ongoing continuous. I guess we have to tweak the language but wanna get your thoughts and feedback on how this could be executed and where in the user UI/UX.

title: SIGNAL CAMPAIN IDEA -USERS
tags: Brand positioning, Marketing Campaign Idea, marketing, Launch Campaigns
notes: Just saw a YouTube video where they were discussing social media, the algorithm, and how these social platforms call the people who use their platform "users." They were saying how the only other area where the term "users" is used is when people sell drugs, and the people who use them are called "users." So, they were saying how social media is the new drug, right, and you are the user. Because of the doom-scrolling, the time-wasting, the negativity, the division, and the algorithm, and the more engagement through enragement and click-baiting and blah blah blah, it feeds that capitalist engine, money, money, money. The more you engage, more money generating, but the whole analogy of there are drug users and there are social media users, all the platforms literally called them "users" when they refer to them, so it's like drug users and there's a drug pushers. I like that for us to use when we post certain blogs or certain articles, even just use that for engagement, virality, because that analogy, that you know, drug users, social media users, that's what the platforms call them, so to kind of get people to go, "My god, this is like, yeah, I am they are controlling me, they are like, I am addicted," you know, and Signal is a solution. So when I use this as part of a whole marketing campaign, you know, theme that we could do.

title: Marketing OS Blueprint - & YouTube
tags: Social, Skills, marketing
notes:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1b9tsd4ckGiGgut98ZjrQiD7d0S6s1-IL/view?usp=drivesdk

https://youtu.be/cHM9QrHURpE?is=OWHMkyPi8RVswzt0

Marketing OS Architecture Blueprint
The 4-Layer System That Tracks All Your Marketing Data Automatically
How to use: This is the exact architecture behind the Marketing OS shown in the video. Use it as a blueprint to build your own version,
starting with just one platform and one data source.
1 Data Sources
Your platforms (YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, email) connected to a data connector that pulls metrics automatically. Zero logins, zero
CSV exports, zero manual work. Set it up once, it runs every morning.
Tools: Windsor.ai, Supermetrics, Make.com, Zapier, or any API connector
▼
2 Storage
Structured files that everything else reads from. Nothing fancy. JSON, YAML, or even a Google Sheet. The point is that your data
lives in one organized place, not scattered across five dashboards.
Tools: JSON/YAML files, Google Sheets, Notion database, Airtable
▼
3 Intelligence (The Dashboard)
Where raw data becomes insight. Growth curves, week-over-week trends, competitor feeds, content velocity, demographics, per-
video performance. This is the hero layer. Open it every morning and know exactly where you stand.
Tools: Custom dashboard (built with Claude Code), Looker Studio, Metabase, even a well-structured Google Sheet
▼
4 Action
Your content pipeline and automation layer. Track every video from Idea to Published. AI skills handle research, scripts, titles,
thumbnails, and repurposing. Insights from Layer 3 drive what you create in Layer 4.
Tools: Airtable, Notion, Claude Code skills, n8n, Make.com
The 6 Metrics That Actually Matter
Views per video
Is this topic/title getting clicks?
Average View Duration %
Is this content holding attention?
Subscribers gained per video
Is this video growing the channel?
Week-over-week view trend
Is the channel growing or declining?
CTR (click-through rate)
Is the thumbnail/title combo working?
Content velocity
Are you publishing enough, consistently?
The test: if a number moved significantly this week, would you change a decision? If not, stop tracking it.
Where to Start
1 Pick one platform.
Don't connect everything on day one. Start with YouTube or wherever your main audience lives.
2 Find your data connector.
Most platforms have an API or a connector tool. If you can get data into a spreadsheet automatically, Layer 1 is done.
3 Build Layer 3 before Layer 2.
A simple dashboard (even a Google Sheet with charts) is more valuable than a perfect storage system nobody looks at.
4 "Good enough to use daily" is the target.
Not perfect. Not comprehensive. Just useful enough that you open it every morning. Then stop building and start creating.
Starter Prompt
Paste this into Claude Code to build your first dashboard:
Build me a simple marketing analytics dashboard.
I post on [YouTube / TikTok / Instagram].
Pull my data from [data source, e.g. a CSV export or Google Sheet].
Show me: views per video, week-over-week trends, and a chart of subscriber growth.
Keep it simple. One HTML file. I want to open it every morning and know where I stand.
